children.heading: Biological product
children.label: 1 Biological product
children.text: The term “biological product” has the meaning given such term in .section 262 of this title

children.heading: Drug; device
children.label: 2 Drug; device
children.text: The terms “drug” and “device” have the meanings given such terms in .section 321 of title 21

children.label: A
children.text: is a priority to diagnose, mitigate, prevent, or treat harm from any disease or condition; and

children.label: B
children.text: for which the incentives of the commercial market are unlikely to result in its adequate or timely development.

children.heading: Medical product
children.label: 4 Medical product
children.text: The term “medical product” means a drug, device, biological product, or product that is a combination of drugs, devices, and biological products.

children.heading: Establishment of the Cures Acceleration Network
children.label: b Establishment of the Cures Acceleration Network

children.text: be under the direction of the Director of the Center, taking into account the recommendations of a CAN Review Board (referred to in this section as the “Board”), described in subsection (d); and

award grants and contracts to eligible entities, as described in subsection (e), to accelerate the development of high need cures, including through the development of medical products and behavioral therapies.
conduct and support revolutionary advances in basic research, translating scientific discoveries from bench to bedside;
award grants and contracts to eligible entities to accelerate the development of high need cures;

provide the resources necessary for government agencies, independent investigators, research organizations, biotechnology companies, academic research institutions, and other entities to develop high need cures;
reduce the barriers between laboratory discoveries and clinical trials for new therapies; and

children.label: A
children.text: the facilitation of regular and ongoing communication with the Food and Drug Administration regarding the status of activities conducted under this section;

children.label: B
children.text: ensuring that such activities are coordinated with the approval requirements of the Food and Drug Administration, with the goal of expediting the development and approval of countermeasures and products; and

children.label: C
children.text: connecting interested persons with additional technical assistance made available under .section 360bbb–4 of title 21

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established a Cures Acceleration Network Review Board (referred to in this section as the “Board”), which shall advise the Director of the Center on the conduct of the activities of the Cures Acceleration Network.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: i Appointment
children.text: The Board shall be comprised of 24 members who are appointed by the Secretary and who serve at the pleasure of the Secretary.

children.heading: Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.label: ii Chairperson and Vice Chairperson
children.text: The Secretary shall designate, from among the 24 members appointed under clause (i), one Chairperson of the Board (referred to in this section as the “Chairperson”) and one Vice Chairperson.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Each member shall be appointed to serve a 4-year term, except that any member appointed to fill a vacancy occurring prior to the expiration of the term for which the member’s predecessor was appointed shall be appointed for the remainder of such term.

children.heading: Consecutive appointments; maximum terms
children.label: ii Consecutive appointments; maximum terms
children.text: A member may be appointed to serve not more than 3 terms on the Board, and may not serve more than 2 such terms consecutively.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint individuals to the Board based solely upon the individual’s established record of distinguished service in one of the areas of expertise described in clause (ii). Each individual appointed to the Board shall be of distinguished achievement and have a broad range of disciplinary interests.

children.label: aa
children.text: basic research;

children.label: bb
children.text: medicine;

children.label: cc
children.text: biopharmaceuticals;

children.label: dd
children.text: discovery and delivery of medical products;

children.label: ee
children.text: bioinformatics and gene therapy;

children.label: ff
children.text: medical instrumentation; and

children.label: gg
children.text: regulatory review and approval of medical products,

children.label: II
children.text: At least 4 individuals shall be recognized leaders in professional venture capital or private equity organizations and have demonstrated experience in private equity investing.

children.label: III
children.text: At least 8 individuals shall represent disease advocacy organizations.

children.label: i
children.text: a representative of the National Institutes of Health, recommended by the Secretary of the Department of Health and Human Services;

children.label: ii
children.text: a representative of the Office of the Assistant Secretary of Defense for Health Affairs, recommended by the Secretary of Defense;

children.label: iii
children.text: a representative of the Office of the Under Secretary for Health for the Veterans Health Administration, recommended by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs;

children.label: iv
children.text: a representative of the National Science Foundation, recommended by the Chair of the National Science Board; and

children.label: v
children.text: a representative of the Food and Drug Administration, recommended by the Commissioner of Food and Drugs.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: B Terms
children.text: Each ex-officio member shall serve a 3-year term on the Board, except that the Chairperson may adjust the terms of the initial ex-officio members in order to provide for a staggered term of appointment for all such members.

children.heading: Responsibilities of the Board and the Director of the Center
children.label: 4 Responsibilities of the Board and the Director of the Center
I
children.text: policies, programs, and procedures for carrying out the duties of the Director of the Center under this section; and

children.label: II
children.text: significant barriers to successful translation of basic science into clinical application (including issues under the purview of other agencies and departments).

children.heading: Report
children.label: ii Report
children.text: In the case that the Board identifies a significant barrier, as described in clause (i)(II), the Board shall submit to the Secretary a report regarding such barrier.

children.heading: Responsibilities of the Director of the Center
children.label: B Responsibilities of the Director of the Center
children.text: With respect to each recommendation provided by the Board under subparagraph (A)(i), the Director of the Center shall respond in writing to the Board, indicating whether such Director will implement such recommendation. In the case that the Director of the Center indicates a recommendation of the Board will not be implemented, such Director shall provide an explanation of the reasons for not implementing such recommendation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall meet 4 times per calendar year, at the call of the Chairperson.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: i Quorum
children.text: A quorum shall consist of a total of 13 members of the Board, excluding ex-officio members, with diverse representation as described in clause (iii).

children.heading: Chairperson or Vice Chairperson
children.label: ii Chairperson or Vice Chairperson
children.text: Each meeting of the Board shall be attended by either the Chairperson or the Vice Chairperson.

children.heading: Diverse representation
children.label: iii Diverse representation
children.text: At each meeting of the Board, there shall be not less than one scientist, one representative of a disease advocacy organization, and one representative of a professional venture capital or private equity organization.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: A Compensation
children.text: Members shall receive compensation at a rate to be fixed by the Chairperson but not to exceed a rate equal to the daily equivalent of the annual rate of basic pay prescribed for level IV of the Executive Schedule under for each day (including travel time) during which the member is engaged in the performance of the duties of the Board. All members of the Board who are officers or employees of the United States shall serve without compensation in addition to that received for their services as officers or employees of the United States.section 5315 of title 5

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: B Travel expenses
children.text: Members of the Board shall be allowed travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for persons employed intermittently by the Federal Government under section 5703(b) of title 5, while away from their homes or regular places of business in the performance of services for the Board.3So in original. does not contain a subsec. (b).3Section 5703 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: promote innovation in technologies supporting the advanced research and development and production of high need cures, including through the development of medical products and behavioral therapies.

children.label: B
children.text: accelerate the development of high need cures, including through the development of medical products, behavioral therapies, and biomarkers that demonstrate the safety or effectiveness of medical products; or

children.label: C
children.text: help the award recipient establish protocols that comply with Food and Drug Administration standards and otherwise permit the recipient to meet regulatory requirements at all stages of development, manufacturing, review, approval, and safety surveillance of a medical product.

children.label: A
children.text: be a public or private entity, which may include a private or public research institution, an institution of higher education, a medical center, a biotechnology company, a pharmaceutical company, a disease advocacy organization, a patient advocacy organization, or an academic research institution;

children.label: i
children.text: a detailed description of the project for which the entity seeks such grant or contract;

children.label: ii
children.text: a timetable for such project;

children.label: aa
children.text: progress in carrying out the project; and

children.label: bb
children.text: compliance with all provisions of this section and conditions of receipt of such grant or contract; and

children.label: II
children.text: a final report at the conclusion of the grant period, describing the outcomes of the project; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of the protocols the entity will follow to comply with Food and Drug Administration standards and regulatory requirements at all stages of development, manufacturing, review, approval, and safety surveillance of a medical product; and

children.label: C
children.text: provide such additional information as the Director of the Center may require.

children.heading: The cures acceleration partnership awards
children.label: A The cures acceleration partnership awards

children.heading: Initial award amount
children.label: i Initial award amount
children.text: Each award under this subparagraph shall be not more than $15,000,000 per project for the first fiscal year for which the project is funded, which shall be payable in one payment.

children.heading: Funding in subsequent fiscal years
children.label: ii Funding in subsequent fiscal years
children.text: An eligible entity receiving an award under clause (i) may apply for additional funding for such project by submitting to the Director of the Center the information required under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2). The Director may fund a project of such eligible entity in an amount not to exceed $15,000,000 for a fiscal year subsequent to the initial award under clause (i).

children.heading: Matching funds
children.label: iii Matching funds
children.text: As a condition for receiving an award under this subsection, an eligible entity shall contribute to the project non-Federal funds in the amount of $1 for every $3 awarded under clauses (i) and (ii), except that the Director of the Center may waive or modify such matching requirement in any case where the Director determines that the goals and objectives of this section cannot adequately be carried out unless such requirement is waived.

children.heading: The cures acceleration grant awards
children.label: B The cures acceleration grant awards

children.heading: Initial award amount
children.label: i Initial award amount
children.text: Each award under this subparagraph shall be not more than $15,000,000 per project for the first fiscal year for which the project is funded, which shall be payable in one payment.

children.heading: Funding in subsequent fiscal years
children.label: ii Funding in subsequent fiscal years
children.text: An eligible entity receiving an award under clause (i) may apply for additional funding for such project by submitting to the Board the information required under subparagraphs (B) and (C) of paragraph (2). The Director of the Center may fund a project of such eligible entity in an amount not to exceed $15,000,000 for a fiscal year subsequent to the initial award under clause (i).

children.heading: The cures acceleration flexible research awards
children.label: C The cures acceleration flexible research awards
children.text: If the Director of the Center determines that the goals and objectives of this section cannot adequately be carried out through a contract, grant, or cooperative agreement, the Director of the Center shall have flexible research authority to use other transactions to fund projects in accordance with the terms and conditions of this section. Awards made under such flexible research authority for a fiscal year shall not exceed 20 percent of the total funds appropriated under subsection (g)(1) for such fiscal year.

children.heading: Suspension of awards for defaults, noncompliance with provisions and plans, and diversion of funds; repayment of funds
children.label: 4 Suspension of awards for defaults, noncompliance with provisions and plans, and diversion of funds; repayment of funds
children.text: The Director of the Center may suspend the award to any entity upon noncompliance by such entity with provisions and plans under this section or diversion of funds.

children.heading: Audits
children.label: 5 Audits
children.text: The Director of the Center may enter into agreements with other entities to conduct periodic audits of the projects funded by grants or contracts awarded under this subsection.

children.heading: Closeout procedures
children.label: 6 Closeout procedures
children.text: At the end of a grant or contract period, a recipient shall follow the closeout procedures under section 74.71 of title 45, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation).

children.heading: Review
children.label: 7 Review
children.text: A determination by the Director of the Center as to whether a drug, device, or biological product is a high need cure (for purposes of subsection (a)(3)) shall not be subject to judicial review.

children.heading: Competitive basis of awards
children.label: f Competitive basis of awards
children.text: Any grant, cooperative agreement, or contract awarded under this section shall be awarded on a competitive basis.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of carrying out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $500,000,000 for fiscal year 2010, and such sums as may be necessary for subsequent fiscal years. Funds appropriated under this section shall be available until expended.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds otherwise appropriated
children.label: 2 Limitation on use of funds otherwise appropriated
children.text: No funds appropriated under this chapter, other than funds appropriated under paragraph (1), may be allocated to the Cures Acceleration Network.
